note: Conformance vectors for the sensitivity-scan vocabulary (issue #150). Each repo is a fresh git repository containing exactly these files; expectations are asserted against the `sensitivity` object of `store_status`. Wire strings are the typed vocabulary at shoreline::session::{SensitivityKind, SensitivitySeverity, SensitivityPolicyOutcome}. Downstream boundaries (e.g. shoreline-relay's egress classification gate) can assert identical per-kind behavior from this file. A file may legitimately trigger more than one kind: expectedKinds lists every shoreline finding the file contributes to.
knownTokenDivergence: shoreline's known_token detector matches the sk-/ghp_/github_pat_/AKIA prefixes only at the start of a token. shoreline-relay's egress gate deliberately matches the prefix anywhere in a token: its threat model is 'the secret must not cross the wire', not 'the token starts with a known prefix'. The embedded-prefix vector pins the divergence — shoreline expects no finding (expectedKinds is empty); the relay expects known_token (relayExpectedKinds).
